title: Set up Seeds tracking and initial Mulch project context
description: Bootstrap Seeds as the active tracker for future roadmap work and record initial Mulch expertise so new agents can understand this repo without rereading the full codebase.
closeReason: Seeds tracking was bootstrapped with initial follow-up issues, and Mulch was primed with foundational project context across architecture, workflow, agents, skills, and testing.

title: Skill Library: copy or derive skills into editable scope
description: Add a Copy Skill flow for package/read-only skills. Target project or global scope, require a new name/description to avoid Pi skill-name collision ambiguity, copy the full directory tree, and rewrite SKILL.md frontmatter. Include user-facing warning that duplicate skill names collide and Pi keeps the first discovered skill.
closeReason: Added Skill Library copy flow: backend copySkill helper/API copies discovered skill trees into project/global editable scopes, rewrites SKILL.md frontmatter, rejects duplicate names, and dashboard exposes Copy Skill dialog with collision warning. Added tests and bun run check passes.

title: Clarify template descriptions are dashboard metadata only
description:
Add UI helper text explaining that Template descriptions are for human dashboard context/search only.

Owning UI module after dashboard refactor: web/features/templates/TemplatesPanel.tsx.

Capability resolution uses Template name, items, audience, and auto-application metadata; descriptions are not injected into agent prompts or passed to child agents.

Acceptance: Skill Template and Extension Template editor/copy clearly says descriptions are dashboard metadata/search context only and are not sent to agents.

title: Skill usage telemetry for loaded/used skills
description:
Track or surface which agents loaded a skill and, if feasible, which skills were actually used during a session.

Relevant UI modules after dashboard refactor: usage summaries may belong in web/features/skill-library for skill-centric inventory and/or web/features/live-agents for Live Agent runtime details. Keep the domain boundary clear: Skill Library is inventory-focused; Live Agents are runtime session state.

Acceptance: users can tell which Live Agents loaded a skill, and any 'used' signal is clearly distinguished from merely loaded/configured.

title: Improve automated coverage for spawn/worktree behavior
description: Add tests or mocks around spawn/worktree behavior where possible without a real Pi+bwrap environment. Current tests/README.md notes spawnAgent, sendToAgent, bwrap isolation, delegation, and worktree cleanup as manual verification only.
closeReason: Added automated coverage for spawn planning, worktree lifecycle/cleanup, and sendToAgent queueing/error behavior; updated test strategy docs. bun run check passes.

title: Skill Library: diff view before saving edits
description:
Add a diff/preview step before saving SKILL.md or supporting skill markdown/text files, using the existing hash guard to avoid overwriting disk changes.

Owning UI module after dashboard refactor: web/features/skill-library/SkillLibraryPanel.tsx.

Acceptance: editing a skill file offers a diff/preview before save; save still uses expectedHash guards; stale hash conflicts are surfaced clearly.

title: Skill validation autofix helpers
description:
Add optional autofix helpers for common skill validation problems such as malformed names, missing descriptions, or scaffold gaps.

Owning UI module after dashboard refactor: web/features/skill-library/SkillLibraryPanel.tsx for Skill Library validation UI; backend fixes should remain guarded by existing skill discovery/editing safety constraints.

Should preserve safety: preview changes before writing.

title: Generate tests and examples for skill scripts
description:
For skills that include executable scripts, add tooling or dashboard affordances to generate example inputs and tests.

Owning UI module after dashboard refactor: web/features/skill-library/SkillLibraryPanel.tsx for skill inventory/preview affordances. Any safe backend generation logic should live outside the dashboard component.

Skill scripts should remain visible/previewable as text; do not add arbitrary dashboard run actions without a separate safety design.

title: Retire ISSUES.md after Seeds migration
description: Remove the legacy ISSUES.md roadmap after migrating actionable follow-up work into Seeds. Keep CLAUDE.md for agent integration context even if Claude is not the primary interface.
closeReason: Removed ISSUES.md after migrating follow-up work into Seeds; kept CLAUDE.md for agent integration context; updated Mulch evidence and recorded the decision.

title: Plan version-controlled Orchestrator libraries
description: Design support for version-controlled Pi Orchestrator resources: agent types, skill templates, extension templates, and optionally bundled skills/extensions. Separate generic Pi resource paths from Orchestrator-specific library paths, and add bootstrap/import UX for users who want a git-backed personal/team library.
closeReason: Planning decisions were captured and implemented through Phase 1 tracer bullets: user-owned namespaced Orchestrator Libraries, configurable manifest resource dirs, settings/discovery/load order, bootstrap backend/API, package example defaults, and follow-up Seeds for remaining GUI/tool work.

title: Orchestrator Library: onboarding, discovery, and package example defaults
description: Implement user-owned Orchestrator Library setup. Do not write orchestrator resources into the package directory. Keep package-shipped defaults read-only and clearly prefixed as examples/fallbacks (e.g. pio-example-*), with dashboard affordances to copy/customize into a configured user/team library.
closeReason: Completed across Phase 1 and package-example follow-ups: dashboard shows Orchestrator Library onboarding/read view, discovers user-owned libraries/resources, package examples remain read-only and pio-example-prefixed, and display settings can hide package examples after user libraries are configured.

title: Orchestrator Library: support multiple configured libraries with precedence
description: Support more than one configured Orchestrator Library at once. Define discovery, precedence, source labeling, duplicate-name handling, and editable target selection across global/project library lists.
closeReason: Completed by configured multi-library discovery/settings work: global and project libraries are read in load order, resources retain source/library metadata, duplicate namespaces are diagnosed, and ordering is persisted through settings.

title: Orchestrator Library: package examples visibility toggle
description: Always ship/show read-only package examples by default, but add a dashboard/settings toggle to hide package example/default resources to reduce clutter after users configure their own libraries.
closeReason: Added project-level piAgentOrchestrator.showPackageExamples display setting, Orchestrator Libraries dashboard toggle, API update route, and agent type filtering for package example resources when disabled. Added tests and ran bun run check.

title: Orchestrator Library: duplicate-name diagnostics and validation rules
description: Define duplicate-name behavior across libraries and generic Pi resources. Require each Orchestrator Library manifest name to be unique and use it as a namespace for resource refs. Agent/template internal IDs can be namespace-qualified, but runtime-facing conflicts must still be validated. Library import should mark incompatible/conflicting resources with diagnostics rather than silently choosing ambiguous entries. Skill/extension duplicate names should produce catalog warnings, and creating/updating a skill template must be blocked if its resolved selected skills contain duplicate runtime skill names. Likewise, spawning an agent should validate the final resolved skill set and fail clearly if conflicting skill names would be loaded.
closeReason: Added runtime skill-name conflict detection to capability resolution and spawn guards in tool/API paths. Conflicting resolved skills now block spawning with a clear error. Existing manifest namespace duplicate diagnostics remain covered. Added focused unit test for duplicate SKILL.md runtime names.

title: Orchestrator Library: editable load order controls
description: Add dashboard controls to reorder configured Orchestrator Libraries with simple up/down buttons. Library order controls precedence: earlier libraries win for agent/template names; later duplicate orchestrator resources are discovered but inactive/conflicted.
closeReason: Completed by Orchestrator Libraries dashboard settings work: configured libraries can be reordered with up/down controls, persisted through the settings API, with UI copy explaining earlier libraries appear first and influence precedence.

title: Orchestrator Library: manifest schema with configurable resource directories
description: Define and validate an orchestrator-library.json manifest. A folder counts as an Orchestrator Library only when it contains a valid manifest. Manifest should support configurable locations for agents, skill templates, extension templates, skills, and extensions so users can organize repos differently while still conforming to a stable schema.
closeReason: Completed by Phase 1 manifest work: orchestrator-library.json parser validates schema/name/compatibility, configurable resource directories for agents/templates/skills/extensions, relative resolution, missing-dir warnings, and path escape rejection with tests.

title: Orchestrator Library: compatibility version checks
description: Add manifest compatibility checks such as schema version and minimum/compatible Pi Orchestrator package version. Warn or block library loading when a library requires a newer incompatible orchestrator version while preserving backward compatibility where possible.

title: Orchestrator Library: future manifest migration/upgrade tooling
description: Parking lot: add an Upgrade Manifest action only when a future library manifest schema introduces meaningful incompatibility. For v1, validate/warn/block as appropriate but do not build migration UI yet.

title: Dashboard GUI: bootstrap new Orchestrator Library
description:
Backend/API bootstrap is already implemented. Remaining work: add a dashboard flow in the Orchestrator Libraries tab to create/register a new user/team Orchestrator Library from an explicit target path. UI should collect target path, library name, optional description, call POST /api/orchestrator-libraries/bootstrap, show validation/errors, refresh configured libraries, and explain that in-repo paths are registered in project settings while outside-repo paths are registered globally. Starter scaffold contents are handled by existing backend.

UX note from discussion: when no Orchestrator Library is configured, the existing dashed/empty-state box should itself be an obvious call to action, e.g. clickable "Click here to scaffold your first Orchestrator Library". The empty state should explain that the program can create a sample/starter library for them rather than leaving users to infer next steps from settings docs.
closeReason: Adjusted bootstrap flow per UX feedback: empty-state CTA and +New Library now open a modal dialog for target path/name/description instead of rendering the form inline. Updated dashboard smoke test and validated with bun run check.

title: Agent tool: create or modify Orchestrator Libraries from explicit paths
description: Expose safe Pi/orchestrator tools so the root orchestrator agent can bootstrap and modify user Orchestrator Libraries. The user must provide an explicit filesystem path for creation. Tools should validate manifest/schema, constrain writes to the selected library root, and create agent types, skills, extensions, and templates in the configured manifest resource directories.

title: Deprecate legacy .pi/agents and template folders in favor of Orchestrator Libraries
description: Replace legacy project-local .pi/agents, .pi/skill-templates, and .pi/extension-templates storage/discovery with Orchestrator Library discovery. Since the app is still pre-release/prototype, prefer the end-state architecture over backwards compatibility. Provide a migration/copy path if local legacy resources exist, but new create/edit flows should target configured libraries only.
closeReason: Moved new agent type and skill/extension template save flows to configured Orchestrator Libraries when present, and added Orchestrator Library discovery for agent definitions and templates. Legacy .pi folders remain readable/fallback for migration/no-library setups. Added focused tests and bun run check passes.

title: Default skill creation into selected Orchestrator Library
description: Update skill creation flows so the user selects a target Orchestrator Library when available, defaulting to the first configured library by load order. If no library is selected/available, allow fallback to generic global Pi skills folder. Store created skills under the target library's manifest-configured skills directory. Do not add dashboard extension authoring; extension creation should be agent-assisted/manual due to executable code and Pi SDK complexity.
closeReason: Updated skill creation so backend can target an explicit Orchestrator Library and defaults to the first configured library when no scope is provided, falling back to global Pi skills when no library exists. Dashboard New Skill dialog now lists configured libraries in load order plus global fallback. Added focused tests and bun run check passes.

title: Agent-assisted extension authoring for Orchestrator Libraries
description:
Do not build a dashboard Add Extension editor. Instead, support extension creation/modification through agent-assisted workflows that can read Pi SDK docs/examples, write code into a selected Orchestrator Library's manifest-configured extensions directory, and rely on normal code review/testing.

Dashboard relationship after feature-module refactor: web/features/orchestrator-libraries should focus on discovering existing Orchestrator Library extensions and web/features/templates should assign them to Extension Templates. Do not add arbitrary extension code authoring to the dashboard UI.

Acceptance: agent-assisted workflow can create/modify extension files inside a selected Orchestrator Library with path guards; dashboard discovers the resulting extension and can assign it through Extension Templates.

title: Resolve Orchestrator Library skills explicitly at agent spawn
description: Do not require Orchestrator Library skills to be added to generic Pi skill settings. Discover library skills for the dashboard/templates, then resolve selected skill/template entries to concrete file paths and pass them to child agents with repeatable `--skill <path>` flags. Use `--no-skills` for template-scoped agents when appropriate so spawned agents receive exactly the skills selected by their agent type/templates. Keep generic Pi skill paths as an optional separate feature for the root/user Pi session.
closeReason: Orchestrator Library skills are now discovered in the Skill Library and namespace refs like team:skills/example-analysis resolve to concrete SKILL.md paths during capability resolution, so spawn receives explicit --skill paths through existing spawnAgent behavior. Added focused tests for discovery and capability resolution.

title: Skill templates store library-relative skill references
description: Change skill templates to store skill references by library-relative path/id rather than bare skill name. Resolve refs relative to the owning Orchestrator Library manifest/resource directories, then pass concrete paths with repeatable `--skill` at agent spawn. This avoids duplicate skill-name ambiguity while preserving user-readable labels in the dashboard.
closeReason: Skill Library now exposes Orchestrator Library skill refs like team:skills/example-analysis/SKILL.md, and template UI/add-to-template uses those refs instead of bare names when available while preserving display labels. Resolution to concrete --skill paths was completed in the previous follow-up. Tests/build/typecheck pass.

title: Orchestrator Library: manifest name as unique namespace
description: Require each Orchestrator Library manifest to declare a unique stable name/namespace. Reject adding a library whose manifest name duplicates an already configured library. Use the library name as the namespace for resource identities and refs (e.g. libraryName:agents/reviewer.md, libraryName:skills/code-review), reducing cross-library conflicts while preserving display names.
closeReason: Completed by manifest namespace implementation: manifest.name is used as the stable unique library namespace, duplicate configured namespaces are diagnosed with both paths, and resource IDs/refs use namespace-qualified identifiers.

title: Extension template smoke test for runtime tool conflicts
description: Add a dashboard smoke test/dry run for extension templates. Spawn a minimal temporary Pi RPC agent with the selected extension template, inspect registered runtime tools via getActiveTools/getAllTools or runtime-tools snapshot, and report tool namespace/name conflicts back to the dashboard. Use this for validation because extension tool registrations are only known at runtime.
closeReason: Implemented extension template smoke-test endpoint and dashboard action. Smoke tests spawn a temporary agent with resolved extensions, read runtime tool snapshot/conflicts, show diagnostics, and clean up worktree; added automated tests and ran bun run check.

title: Log runtime tool conflicts from spawned agents
description: When spawned agents report runtime tool snapshots, detect duplicate/conflicting tool names or namespaces where possible and surface diagnostics in logs/dashboard. This complements extension template smoke tests and helps identify conflicts that only appear during runtime.
closeReason: Added runtime tool conflict logging via logRuntimeToolConflicts when agent snapshots are serialized/inspected, surfaced conflict badges in live agent cards and conflict lines in inspect output, and added runtime-tools coverage for duplicate registration source labels. bun run check passes.

title: Phase 1: Orchestrator Library foundation tracer bullets
description: Implement the narrow, testable foundation for user-owned Orchestrator Libraries before dashboard editing flows. Keep changes small and independently validated: manifest parsing, unique namespace validation, settings storage, discovery from one/multiple libraries, onboarding read state, and bootstrap scaffold.
closeReason: Completed all Phase 1 tracer bullets: manifest parsing, unique namespaces, settings, single/multiple library discovery, dashboard read/onboarding view, bootstrap scaffold, package examples, and reorder controls. Full bun run check passes.

title: Phase 1.2: Enforce unique Orchestrator Library namespaces
description: Use manifest.name as the unique library namespace. Add validation for multiple configured library roots so duplicate manifest names are rejected/diagnosed before resource discovery. Validation: unit tests with two distinct libraries accepted and duplicate names producing a clear diagnostic containing both paths.
closeReason: Added readOrchestratorLibraries() validation that enforces unique manifest namespaces and reports duplicate diagnostics with both library paths. Added unit tests for distinct and duplicate namespaces. Typecheck passes.

title: Phase 1.3: Read/write Orchestrator Library settings
description: Add namespaced settings support under piAgentOrchestrator.libraries in global and project .pi settings while preserving unrelated settings. Support string or object entries if useful, but normalize to a common internal representation. Validation: tests for reading empty settings, reading global+project libraries in order, writing project libraries, writing global libraries, preserving packages/skills/extensions settings.
closeReason: Added piAgentOrchestrator.libraries settings read/write helpers with normalized global/project library entries, string/object support, unrelated setting preservation, and unit tests for empty/read/write cases. Typecheck passes.

title: Phase 1.1: Add Orchestrator Library manifest parser
description: Create a small manifest parser/validator for orchestrator-library.json. Validate schema string, required name, optional description, compatibility object, and configurable resource directory fields. Resolve resource paths relative to the manifest directory and reject path escapes. Validation: unit tests for valid manifest, missing manifest, invalid JSON, missing name/schema, missing resource dirs as warnings, and ../ path escape as error.
closeReason: Added orchestrator-library manifest parser/validator with unit coverage for valid manifests, missing/invalid manifests, missing required fields, missing resource dirs as warnings, and path escape rejection. Typecheck passes.

title: Phase 1.6: Add dashboard Orchestrator Library setup/read view
description: Add a dashboard settings/onboarding view that shows configured Orchestrator Libraries, validation status, manifest metadata, discovered resource counts, and diagnostics. If no library is configured, show a clear setup prompt. No create/edit resource flows yet. Validation: dashboard smoke test passes; API endpoint returns empty state and configured-library state.
closeReason: Added /api/orchestrator-libraries read endpoint and dashboard Orchestrator Libraries tab showing empty setup state, configured libraries, counts, validity, and diagnostics. Dashboard build, smoke test, endpoint-adjacent tests, and typecheck pass.

title: Phase 1.5: Discover multiple libraries in load order
description: Combine global and project Orchestrator Libraries into a load-ordered discovery result. Earlier configured libraries appear first. Do not implement editing yet. Package example handling is tracked separately in Phase 1.9. Validation: tests prove ordered results and duplicate library namespace diagnostics.
closeReason: Added configured multi-library discovery from global+project settings in load order, with duplicate namespace diagnostics and tests for ordered resources plus duplicate handling. Package examples remain tracked separately in Phase 1.9. Typecheck passes.

title: Phase 1.4: Discover resources from one Orchestrator Library
description: Discover agent types, skill templates, extension templates, skills, and extensions from a single valid Orchestrator Library using the manifest resource directories. Return source metadata with library namespace, library path, resource type, file path, editable status, and read-only/error diagnostics. Validation: fixture library with one resource per category is discovered; missing directories produce warnings not crashes.
closeReason: Added single-library resource discovery for agents, skill templates, extension templates, skills, and extensions with library metadata/editability and diagnostics. Added tests for one resource per category and missing directories as warnings. Typecheck passes.

title: Phase 1.8: Reorder configured Orchestrator Libraries with up/down controls
description: Add simple up/down controls in the dashboard settings view to reorder configured Orchestrator Libraries within their settings scope. Persist the new order and refresh discovery. Validation: API/unit test for reordering array entries; dashboard smoke test; UI copy explains earlier libraries appear first and influence defaults/diagnostics.
closeReason: Added PUT /api/orchestrator-libraries/settings and dashboard up/down controls for configured library order with explanatory copy. Existing settings tests cover reorder persistence via array writes; dashboard smoke/build and typecheck pass.

title: Phase 1.9: Rename package defaults to pio-example-* and mark read-only
description: Rename/adjust package-shipped default agents/templates to clearly use pio-example-* naming and ensure discovery metadata marks package resources as read-only examples. Keep examples visible by default. Validation: agent/template discovery lists pio-example-* package examples with read-only/example metadata and no dashboard save/delete action targets package files.
closeReason: Renamed package agent defaults to pio-example-* and added package definition metadata readOnly/example. Added test ensuring package definitions are read-only pio examples. Typecheck passes.

title: Phase 1.7: Bootstrap starter Orchestrator Library scaffold
description: Add a bootstrap backend/API flow that requires an explicit target path, creates orchestrator-library.json, README, standard resource folders, and one example resource per category using example-* names. Auto-register in project settings if the resolved path is inside the current repo; otherwise register in global settings. Optional git init can be deferred unless trivial. Validation: tests for scaffold contents, path scope classification, refusal to overwrite non-empty incompatible dirs, and resulting manifest passes parser.
closeReason: Added bootstrapOrchestratorLibrary backend and POST /api/orchestrator-libraries/bootstrap. Scaffold creates manifest, README, standard folders, and example resources, auto-registers project vs global by target path scope, and refuses incompatible non-empty dirs. Unit tests and typecheck pass.

title: Evaluate Context Mode as optional heavy-output extension template
description:
Evaluate mksglu/context-mode as an optional Orchestrator Library Extension Template for agents that produce or inspect large outputs.

Relevant modules after dashboard refactor: backend extension discovery/template smoke tests remain under extensions/multi-agent; dashboard assignment/smoke-test UI belongs in web/features/templates; Orchestrator Library discovery/status belongs in web/features/orchestrator-libraries.

Acceptance criteria:
- Context Mode can be discovered as a Pi extension or assigned via an Orchestrator Library Extension Template.
- Extension Template smoke test reports ctx_* runtime tools and no conflicts with delegate/multi-agent tools.
- A spawned test agent can use ctx_execute or ctx_execute_file successfully.
- Verify Context Mode session DB/cache paths do not pollute repo state or conflict across worktrees.
- Verify Context Mode SQLite/session storage remains clearly separate from Seeds (.seeds/issues.jsonl) and Mulch (.mulch/expertise/*.jsonl).
- Document recommended use: opt-in for researcher/test/log/browser-heavy agents, not default for all agents unless measured overhead is acceptable.
- Document boundaries: Seeds remains issue/task source of truth; Mulch remains curated durable expertise; Context Mode is short-term session continuity and large-output sandboxing.

title: Skill Library: clear stale detail controls immediately on skill selection
description: When selecting a different skill, the detail pane briefly shows stale files and Edit/Delete controls from the previously selected skill until the new detail fetch resolves. This can allow actions to target the old skill while the title/list selection reflects the new one. Clear stale detail/tree/file state immediately on selection change and disable/hide destructive controls until loaded detail matches the selected skill.
closeReason: Fixed transient stale Skill Library detail state by clearing detail/tree/file/edit state immediately on selection changes, showing a skeleton while loading, and only rendering edit/delete/save controls when loaded detail.skill.id matches the current selected skill. bun run check passes.

title: Add Playwright-style dashboard interaction tests
description:
Evaluate adding Playwright (or an equivalent browser automation harness) so agents can exercise dashboard flows end-to-end.

Relevant feature modules after dashboard refactor: web/features/orchestrator-libraries, web/features/skill-library, web/features/templates, web/features/agent-types, plus Dashboard Shell wiring in web/app.tsx.

Include tests for transient selection/loading states, destructive action targeting, Skill Library copy/edit/delete flows, Template interactions, Agent Type interactions, and Orchestrator Library management. Keep this separate from lightweight dashboard smoke tests.

title: Orchestrator Libraries: show loading skeleton for async panel content
description: When opening the Orchestrator Libraries tab, the card header/top copy renders first and the lower async content (package example resources toggle, empty configured-library state, diagnostics/resources) appears a moment later. Add a skeleton/loading placeholder for the panel content while /api/orchestrator-libraries is loading so the card layout is stable and content does not pop in abruptly.
closeReason: Added loading skeleton placeholder in the Orchestrator Libraries panel while /api/orchestrator-libraries is loading and no data has rendered yet, preventing lower content from popping in abruptly. bun run check passes.

title: Deprecate Skill & Extension Paths dashboard tab in favor of Orchestrator Libraries
description: Hide/de-emphasize Pi native skill/extension source path settings in favor of Orchestrator Libraries as the primary product abstraction. Do not delete backend/resource-settings code yet; keep it as an advanced/native Pi escape hatch while we learn whether it is still needed. Remove/hide the first-class 'Skill & Extension Paths' dashboard tab from primary navigation, update UI/docs to direct users to Orchestrator Libraries for orchestrator-managed agents/templates/skills/extensions, and prioritize Orchestrator Library discovery in picker/template flows. Extension discovery should include/consolidate Orchestrator Library extensions for extension templates; native project/global extension paths and npm package extensions can remain fallback/advanced/package sources. Acceptance: dashboard primary flow no longer encourages manual skill/extension paths for orchestrator resources; any remaining access is clearly labeled advanced/native Pi settings.
closeReason: Removed Skill & Extension Paths from primary dashboard navigation, exposed native Pi resource paths only as an advanced escape hatch under Orchestrator Libraries, and added Orchestrator Library extension discovery for extension templates. Validated with bun run check.

title: Label package-provided skills in Skill Library
description: Add clear dashboard labeling for skills that come from installed Pi packages/package-manager paths (for example paths under ~/.pi/agent/npm or bun/package node_modules, such as the librarian skill). In the Skill Library list/detail views, show a small 'package' chip/badge so users can distinguish externally installed package-provided skills from project/global/user-created skills. Acceptance: package-provided skills are detected consistently from discovery metadata/path/source; the badge is visible in list and/or detail views without changing editability semantics; global/project skills remain labeled as global/project and are not confused with package skills.
closeReason: Implemented packageProvided skill detection for package-manager paths/source metadata and rendered a 'package' badge in Skill Library list/detail views. Validated with bun run check.

title: Add spawnable agent classes and reserve orchestrator for root session
description:
Introduce an explicit distinction between spawnable child agent classes and the root orchestrator role. The interactive Pi session becomes the orchestrator via /orchestrate; users should not create/spawn child agents that pretend to be orchestrators. Proposed spawnable classes: lead, scout, implementer, reviewer. Reserve orchestrator as a non-spawnable root/session role.

Root orchestrator profile direction: support root-only skills/instructions/prompts from Orchestrator Libraries, but do NOT auto-load arbitrary root extensions from libraries. The root shell already runs the pi-agent-orchestrator extension; additional root extension behavior should come from normal trusted Pi extension installation or a fork/custom package. Library extensions remain for spawned agents via --extension.

Acceptance direction:
- Agent definitions support an explicit class/category field for spawnable roles.
- Dashboard create/edit flows use a class picker and do not offer orchestrator as spawnable.
- Backend spawn paths reject reserved/non-spawnable orchestrator definitions even if manually edited.
- Package pio-example-orchestrator is hidden from spawnable agent type lists or recast as root-profile/example documentation.
- Skill templates and possibly skills can carry audience/scope metadata such as spawned/orchestrator/all.
- Capability resolution refuses orchestrator-only resources for child agents.
- Root orchestrator profiles can contribute skills/instructions/prompts, likely through reload/resource discovery or before_agent_start injection.
- Root orchestrator profiles do not load library extension files into the interactive shell.
- Root orchestrator access model is documented separately from child --skill/--extension injection.
closeReason: Verified spawnable agent classes epic acceptance is implemented: agent definitions parse/save class metadata, dashboard create/edit uses spawnable-only class picker, backend/API/tool/spawn paths reject orchestrator-class definitions, package orchestrator example is non-spawnable, template/skill audience guardrails and root profile capability resolution are in place, README/CONTEXT document root profiles separately, and bun run check passes.

title: Implement root orchestrator profiles for skills and instructions
description:
Follow-up under pi-agent-orchestrator-9c53. Add a root orchestrator profile concept for the interactive /orchestrate session. Profiles may contribute root-only skills/prompts/instructions from Orchestrator Libraries, likely via persisted orchestration mode/profile selection plus ctx.reload/resources_discover and/or before_agent_start injection. Do not load arbitrary library extension files into the root shell.

Design notes from discussion:
- The package should always ship/discover a built-in root profile named `default`; there should never be a no-profile state.
- Users may copy/create additional root profiles later.
- `/orchestrate <profile>` should activate the named root orchestrator profile without prompting.
- If `/orchestrate` is run with no profile argument and exactly one root profile exists, activate that profile automatically. In practice this will usually be the built-in `default` profile.
- If `/orchestrate` is run with no profile argument and multiple root profiles exist, do not pick default/first silently; ask the user to select which profile to use.
- Root profiles are for orchestrator skills/instructions only, not arbitrary extension loading.

Acceptance: /orchestrate can activate a selected/default root profile; profile instructions/skills are available to the root session after activation/reload; root profile behavior is documented separately from spawned child --skill/--extension injection; multiple-profile no-argument flow prompts for selection instead of assuming a default; built-in `default` root profile is always available.
closeReason: Implemented root orchestrator profiles. Added built-in default profile, profile discovery from package/user/project/Orchestrator Library directories, target-aware profile capability resolution, /orchestrate profile activation with multi-profile selection, persisted profile state across reloads, resources_discover skill injection, before_agent_start instruction injection, Orchestrator Library starter scaffold support, and README docs. Validation: bun run check passes (87 tests).

title: Add audience guardrails for orchestrator-only skills and templates
description:
Follow-up under pi-agent-orchestrator-9c53. Add audience/scope metadata for skills and skill templates such as spawned/orchestrator/all, then enforce that orchestrator-only resources cannot be attached to spawned child agents.

Design notes from discussion:
- `applyToAll` currently means auto-apply to newly spawned agents; rename/represent this more explicitly as auto-apply to all spawned agents.
- Keep audience/eligibility separate from auto-application.
- Skill templates should support audience/eligibility for spawned agents, root orchestrator, or both.
- Skill templates should support automatic application modes: manual/specific assignment only, all spawned agents, or all/everywhere including orchestrator.
- The UI should use a radio/select-style control rather than one ambiguous boolean checkbox.
- Specific agent-type assignment remains done from the agent type editor by assigning a skill template.
- Backend validation must enforce impossible states even if bypassing the UI/API clients.
- Extension templates remain spawned-agent-only for now; no root orchestrator extension loading.

Acceptance: dashboard labels/filtering make orchestrator-only resources clear; template save/edit supports audience metadata; capability resolution refuses orchestrator-only skills/templates for child spawn; root orchestrator profiles may use orchestrator-only skills/instructions; tests cover manual markdown bypass attempts.
closeReason: Implemented template audience/autoApply metadata and guardrails. Skill templates now separate eligibility from auto-application; extension templates remain spawned-only. Capability resolution is target-aware and rejects orchestrator-only templates/skills for spawned agents, with server spawn API enforcing resolution errors. Dashboard template editor uses audience/automatic-application selects, labels templates and skill audiences, and hides orchestrator-only templates from spawned agent type assignment. README/starter scaffold updated; bun run check passes.

title: Skill Library: synchronize markdown preview and raw scroll positions
description:
Add synchronized scrolling between markdown preview and raw/source panes when the Skill Library shows them side by side, so users do not need to manually align both views.

Owning UI module after dashboard refactor: web/features/skill-library/SkillLibraryPanel.tsx.

Consider proportional scroll sync based on scrollTop/(scrollHeight-clientHeight) rather than line-perfect mapping, with guards to avoid scroll event feedback loops.

Acceptance: preview/raw panes stay roughly aligned while scrolling either pane; sync can be disabled or should not fight user interaction; works for SKILL.md and supporting markdown/text files; dashboard smoke test or focused UI/unit coverage verifies no runtime errors.

title: Dashboard GUI: manage root orchestrator profiles
description:
Add dashboard support for Root Orchestrator Profiles now that /orchestrate can activate profile markdown. Canonical language: Root Orchestrator Profiles are root-only and are not Agent Types.

Likely UI domain after dashboard refactor: add a new domain module such as web/features/root-orchestrator-profiles, with Dashboard Shell wiring in web/app.tsx and links/counts from web/features/orchestrator-libraries where useful.

UI should surface discovered profiles including the built-in default, user/project profiles, and Orchestrator Library profiles; show source/read-only/editable status; preview instructions/frontmatter; create/copy/edit/delete editable profiles in Orchestrator Libraries where appropriate; and make it clear profiles contribute root-only skills/instructions, not extensions. Consider adding profile counts/links in the Orchestrator Libraries tab and profile selection/status visibility near orchestration controls.

Acceptance: dashboard can list and inspect Root Orchestrator Profiles; editable library profiles can be created/edited safely with hash/read-only guards or existing dashboard editing conventions; built-in default is visible but read-only; docs/UI copy explain that /orchestrate without an argument prompts when multiple profiles exist.
closeReason: Implemented Root Orchestrator Profiles dashboard management. Added root profile backend detail/save/copy/delete APIs with read-only/hash guards, Orchestrator Library target support, a dedicated web/features/root-orchestrator-profiles panel, Dashboard Shell tab/wiring, shared UI types, README dashboard capability docs, and smoke/API/root-profile tests. Validation: bun run check passes (90 tests).

title: Dashboard GUI: interactive test session for agent types
description:
Add a Test/Play action next to Edit on each Agent Type card. Clicking it should launch a disposable Pi session/agent for that Agent Type and open an embedded pop-up/terminal/chat panel so the user can inspect the initial launch and send test messages. Closing the pop-up should terminate the test agent/session and clean up any worktree/runtime resources.

Owning UI module after dashboard refactor: web/features/agent-types/AgentTypesPanel.tsx. Runtime spawn/RPC plumbing remains in extensions/multi-agent server/spawn/runtime modules.

Design notes:
- The Agent Types list should show a small play/test button next to Edit for each spawnable Agent Type.
- The test session should make it easy to verify prompt, skills, Templates, tools, and extension startup behavior before using the Agent Type in real orchestration.
- This should be disposable: no lingering agent after the test dialog closes.
- Prefer reusing existing spawn/RPC/runtime-tool plumbing where possible, but keep it clearly separate from persistent orchestrator-created Live Agents if that makes cleanup safer.
- If the agent exits immediately, surface startup logs/runtime diagnostics in the dialog.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can start a test session for an Agent Type from the dashboard.
- User can send at least one message and see the response/stream in the dialog.
- Closing/cancelling the dialog kills the test agent and removes temporary resources.
- Runtime diagnostics/tool conflicts are visible enough to debug bad Templates/extensions.
- Non-spawnable Root Orchestrator Profiles are not shown as Agent Type test targets.
closeReason: Implemented Agent Type Test/Play flow: dashboard cards now expose Test, a disposable test dialog can start a session, send a message, show runtime tool diagnostics/conflicts/stderr/worktree status, and close with cleanup. Added backend test-session APIs that spawn outside live agents, reject non-spawnable orchestrator definitions, resolve capabilities, relay messages, and kill/remove worktree on close. Added server coverage and bun run check passes.

title: Skill Library: copy skills into Orchestrator Libraries
description:
Extend the Skill Library Copy Skill dialog target selector beyond project/global Pi skill scopes to include configured editable Orchestrator Libraries.

Owning UI module after dashboard refactor: web/features/skill-library/SkillLibraryPanel.tsx. Backend copy behavior lives in extensions/multi-agent/skill-discovery.ts and server endpoints.

The target dropdown should list each valid writable Orchestrator Library by manifest name with its path in parentheses, and copy the skill into that library's manifest-configured skills directory. Keep existing collision safeguards and frontmatter rewrite behavior.

Acceptance: with one or more editable Orchestrator Libraries configured, Copy Skill offers each library as a target; copied skills appear under the library skills resource directory; duplicate skill names are still rejected across discovered skills; invalid/read-only libraries are disabled or omitted with a clear explanation.

title: Dashboard refreshes dependent data after Orchestrator Library changes
description:
After bootstrapping or changing Orchestrator Libraries, dashboard tabs can show stale data until the whole page is refreshed. Example: scaffolded library's example-analysis skill did not appear in Skill Library until browser refresh.

Owning areas after dashboard refactor: Dashboard Shell refresh wiring in web/app.tsx plus dependent feature modules web/features/orchestrator-libraries, web/features/skill-library, web/features/templates, and web/features/agent-types.

Refresh orchestration should invalidate/reload dependent dashboard data when libraries change and/or when navigating to tabs whose data depends on library discovery: Skill Library, Agent Types, Templates, Extensions, and Root Orchestrator Profiles as applicable.

Acceptance: after bootstrap/remove/path edit, navigating to Skill Library shows newly discovered library skills without page reload; Templates/Agent Types/Extensions lists similarly reflect current library configuration; refresh avoids excessive polling but reliably runs on tab activation or explicit library-change callbacks.

title: Templates dashboard: choose Orchestrator Library target explicitly
description:
Add explicit Orchestrator Library target selection when creating/saving skill templates and extension templates from the dashboard.

Owning UI module after dashboard refactor: web/features/templates/TemplatesPanel.tsx. Backend/template persistence remains in extensions/multi-agent template modules.

Today Templates may default to a configured library but the user cannot intentionally choose which Orchestrator Library owns the Template. UI should list editable valid libraries by manifest name with path in parentheses, plus legacy project fallback only when appropriate.

Acceptance: new Template dialogs expose a target selector when libraries exist; users can choose among multiple Orchestrator Libraries; saved Template files land in the selected library's manifest-configured skill-templates/extension-templates directory; editing existing Templates preserves their current source/path and does not silently move them unless an explicit move/copy action is designed.

title: Dashboard GUI: edit/remove configured Orchestrator Libraries
description:
Add Orchestrator Libraries dashboard controls to manage configured library registrations after bootstrap.

Owning UI module after dashboard refactor: web/features/orchestrator-libraries/OrchestratorLibrariesPanel.tsx. This feature also owns the nested Native Pi Resource Settings escape hatch.

Users should be able to remove/unregister a library from global/project settings, edit or replace the configured path when a library has moved, and see clear warnings/errors when a saved path no longer exists or no longer contains a valid orchestrator-library.json. Destructive copy should distinguish unregistering from deleting files on disk; do not delete the library directory unless a separate explicit/safe design is added.

Acceptance: each Orchestrator Library card exposes safe edit path/remove registration actions; missing paths are visibly warned with the saved path and settings scope; saving preserves order and scope; settings reload reflects changes without page refresh.

title: Refactor dashboard into vertical feature modules
description:
Epic: incrementally refactor web/app.tsx into vertical feature modules with clear domain names so agents can quickly find the relevant UI code. This was implemented with tracer-bullet child Seeds: Live Agents, Orchestrator Libraries, Agent Types, Skill Library, Templates, and Seeds audit.

Canonical feature/domain modules now used:
- web/features/live-agents: Live Agents and hierarchy UI.
- web/features/agent-types: Agent Types and type editor UI.
- web/features/skill-library: Skill Library inventory/create/copy/edit dialogs.
- web/features/templates: skill/extension Templates UI.
- web/features/orchestrator-libraries: Orchestrator Libraries plus nested Native Pi Resource Settings UI.
- web/shared: common dashboard types/helpers only when genuinely shared.

Dashboard Shell responsibilities remain in web/app.tsx: tabs, SSE/live session state, top-level refresh wiring, shared datasets, and cross-feature dialog orchestration where appropriate.

Epic acceptance:
- web/app.tsx is now a Dashboard Shell instead of a monolithic feature container.
- Feature panels/dialogs live under domain-named modules that match CONTEXT.md language.
- Imports avoid cyclic feature dependencies.
- Existing behavior and bundle entry remain unchanged.
- Follow-up Seeds have been audited/updated so issue descriptions point agents to the right feature domain after the refactor.
closeReason: Completed vertical dashboard feature-module refactor epic through tracer Seeds: Live Agents, Orchestrator Libraries, Agent Types, Skill Library, Templates, and Seeds audit. Each implementation tracer passed bun run check.
